coredata.dc:title:
Preparation and Electrochemical Performance of Modified LiNi
0.5
Mn
1.5
O
4
Cathode Material for Lithium-ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2016 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Tong, Xiaoling
Zeng, Min
Li, Jing
Xu, Hui
Du, Meili
Li, Fuyun
coredata.dc:description: Modified LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4-based materials with Cr-doped (LiNi0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4) and additional lithium (Li1.1Ni0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4) have been synthesized by a solid-state method and their physicochemical and electrochemical performances have been investigated. The experimental results show that the Cr doping in LiNi0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 and Li1.1Ni0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 suppresses the generation of Mn3+, decreases the polarization, improves the conductivity and optimizes the cyclic performance and rate stability compared to the pristine LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4. Besides, Li1.1Ni0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 delivers a higher discharge capacity of 108 mAh g−1 for the first cycle and 99 mAh g−1 after 50 cycles, which is much better than LiNi0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 and LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4. All these results show the vital role of the synergic effects between Cr3+ doping and additional lithium on the enhancement of electrochemical performance for LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4, which will guide the development of LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4-based cathode material in the future.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium-ion batteries
Cr-doped
additional lithium
LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4 cathode

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2016 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Preparation and Electrochemical Performance of Modified LiNi 0.5 Mn 1.5 O 4 Cathode Material for Lithium-ion Batteries Xiaoling Tong, Min Zeng, zengmin@swust.edu.cn Jing Li, Hui Xu, Meili Du, Fuyun Li, School of Materials Science and Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, P R, China School of Materials Science and Engineering Southwest University of Science and Technology Mianyang 621010 P R China School of Materials Science and Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, P R China Modified LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 -based materials with Cr-doped (LiNi -based materials with Cr-doped (LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.4 Cr Cr0.1 O O4 ) and additional lithium (Li ) and additional lithium (Li1.1 Ni Ni0.5 Mn Mn1.4 Cr Cr0.1 O O4 ) have been synthesized by a solid-state method and their physicochemical and electrochemical performances have been investigated. The experimental results show that the Cr doping in LiNi ) have been synthesized by a solid-state method and their physicochemical and electrochemical performances have been investigated. The experimental results show that the Cr doping in LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.4 Cr Cr0.1 O O4 and Li and Li1.1 Ni Ni0.5 Mn Mn1.4 Cr Cr0.1 O O4 suppresses the generation of Mn suppresses the generation of Mn3+ , decreases the polarization, improves the conductivity and optimizes the cyclic performance and rate stability compared to the pristine LiNi , decreases the polarization, improves the conductivity and optimizes the cyclic performance and rate stability compared to the pristine LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 . Besides, Li . Besides, Li1.1 Ni Ni0.5 Mn Mn1.4 Cr Cr0.1 O O4 delivers a higher discharge capacity of 108 mAh g delivers a higher discharge capacity of 108 mAh gâˆ’1 for the first cycle and 99 mAh g for the first cycle and 99 mAh gâˆ’1 after 50 cycles, which is much better than LiNi after 50 cycles, which is much better than LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.4 Cr Cr0.1 O O4 and LiNi and LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 . All these results show the vital role of the synergic effects between Cr . All these results show the vital role of the synergic effects between Cr3+ doping and additional lithium on the enhancement of electrochemical performance for LiNi doping and additional lithium on the enhancement of electrochemical performance for LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 , which will guide the development of LiNi , which will guide the development of LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 -based cathode material in the future.Keywords Lithium-ion batteries Cr-doped additional lithium LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 cathode References 1. Graff, Z. M. Nie, D. W. Choi, J. Liu, J. G. Zhang and M. S. Whittingham, Adv. Mater., 24 (2012) 2109. 28. X. Fang, M.Y. Ge, J.P. Rong, C.W. Zhou, ACS Nano 8: (2014) 4876-X. Fang, M. Y. Ge, J. P. Rong and C. W. Zhou, ACS Nano, 8 (2014) 4876. 29. J.C. Li, Q.L. Zhang, X.C. Xiao, Y.T. Cheng, C.D. Liang,

ce:title.ce:bold: Preparation and Electrochemical Performance of Modified LiNi

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Materials Science and Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, P R, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Materials Science and Engineering
Southwest University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Materials Science and Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, P R China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Modified LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4-based materials with Cr-doped (LiNi0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4) and additional lithium (Li1.1Ni0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4) have been synthesized by a solid-state method and their physicochemical and electrochemical performances have been investigated. The experimental results show that the Cr doping in LiNi0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 and Li1.1Ni0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 suppresses the generation of Mn3+, decreases the polarization, improves the conductivity and optimizes the cyclic performance and rate stability compared to the pristine LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4. Besides, Li1.1Ni0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 delivers a higher discharge capacity of 108 mAh g−1 for the first cycle and 99 mAh g−1 after 50 cycles, which is much better than LiNi0.5Mn1.4Cr0.1O4 and LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4. All these results show the vital role of the synergic effects between Cr3+ doping and additional lithium on the enhancement of electrochemical performance for LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4, which will guide the development of LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4-based cathode material in the future.
